title: App Launch Campaign
context: Launch the new FitPulse fitness app. Multiple stakeholders have contributed requirements and the brief is a mess. Sort it out.

requirements.text: Drive viral organic growth through social media
requirements.explanation: Organic growth is the primary channel strategy — it's the core requirement.

requirements.text: Invest heavily in paid advertising on all major platforms
requirements.explanation: Organic-first and paid-first are fundamentally different strategies requiring different budgets, timelines, and creative.

requirements.text: Target audience: 18–25 year old fitness enthusiasts
requirements.explanation: Clear audience definition is essential — it drives all creative and channel decisions.

requirements.text: Also target 45–60 year olds who rarely use smartphones
requirements.explanation: 18–25 digital natives and 45–60 low-smartphone users need completely different messages, channels, and UX — you can't serve both with one launch campaign.

requirements.text: Campaign should feel inspiring and aspirational
requirements.explanation: Aspirational tone is appropriate for a fitness app aimed at younger users.

requirements.text: Focus on data, science, and clinical proof points
requirements.explanation: Scientific credibility is valuable but secondary to the aspirational hook — it can support the main message.

requirements.text: KPI: 10,000 downloads in the first month
requirements.explanation: A clear, measurable download target is a valid KPI for an app launch.

requirements.text: Include an affiliate marketing programme from day one
requirements.explanation: Affiliate programmes can drive growth but require significant setup — better as a phase 2 initiative.

teachingMoment: Incompatible audience segments are the most common brief failure — trying to speak to everyone means speaking to no one. Define one primary audience.
